steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank follows "Interestingly," and is followed by "It first appears in the poem The Faerie Queene..." — a separate, independent sentence. The blank must itself be a grammatically complete statement that ends with appropriate punctuation. The surrounding context is factual and declarative, not interrogative.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — it was invented by an author?: statement word order but ends with a question mark — mixing declarative structure with interrogative punctuation. ✗
  • Choice B — it was invented by an author.: statement word order with a period — correctly forms a declarative sentence. ✓
  • Choice C — was it invented by an author.: inverted (question) word order with a period — questions must end with question marks, not periods. ✗
  • Choice D — wasn't it invented by an author.: same error as C — inverted word order paired with a period instead of a question mark. ✗
That matches Choice B — it was invented by an author..
